raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":1.0,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.7,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.5","R1.6","R1.7","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"Reviewing the section centrally includes assessing whether the thesis, gap, and contribution framing are coherent at the section level.","R1.2":"The delegation makes internal argument validity load-bearing because the artifact contains multiple linked optimization claims and conclusions.","R1.3":"The section makes claims about empirical verification and reported rates, so review should check whether stated evidence supports those claims as presented.","R1.4":"The artifact asserts a first-rate result and comparison to prior regimes, making novelty positioning a central review responsibility.","R1.5":"Because the request targets a full section, reviewing section-level ordering and narrative progression is centrally expected.","R1.6":"A general section review includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, register, and concision.","R1.7":"The section cites prior work and makes attribution-sensitive claims, so citation coverage and reference use are central responsibilities.","RX.1":"The projection must preserve uncertainty about what can be judged from the excerpt alone.","RX.2":"The review responsibility must avoid endorsing broad or unsupported claims beyond the artifact.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within review projection rather than performing edits or executing the requested review.","RX.4":"Potential downstream harms from misleading technical validation or overconfident scholarship claims must be avoided.","RX.5":"The projection should keep responsibility traceable to explicit features of the delegation and artifact."}}

rationale.R1.1: Reviewing the section centrally includes assessing whether the thesis, gap, and contribution framing are coherent at the section level.
rationale.R1.2: The delegation makes internal argument validity load-bearing because the artifact contains multiple linked optimization claims and conclusions.
rationale.R1.3: The section makes claims about empirical verification and reported rates, so review should check whether stated evidence supports those claims as presented.
rationale.R1.4: The artifact asserts a first-rate result and comparison to prior regimes, making novelty positioning a central review responsibility.
rationale.R1.5: Because the request targets a full section, reviewing section-level ordering and narrative progression is centrally expected.
rationale.R1.6: A general section review includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, register, and concision.
rationale.R1.7: The section cites prior work and makes attribution-sensitive claims, so citation coverage and reference use are central responsibilities.
rationale.RX.1: The projection must preserve uncertainty about what can be judged from the excerpt alone.
rationale.RX.2: The review responsibility must avoid endorsing broad or unsupported claims beyond the artifact.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within review projection rather than performing edits or executing the requested review.
rationale.RX.4: Potential downstream harms from misleading technical validation or overconfident scholarship claims must be avoided.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should keep responsibility traceable to explicit features of the delegation and artifact.
